mission.title: cert-manager: Support Azure Private DNS Zones for DNS Challenge
mission.description: Support Azure Private DNS Zones for DNS Challenge. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
We use cert-manager in air-gapped kubernetes setup for issuing certificates.
We run Azure AKS clusters and have a private ACME server providing us with certificates.
The DNS records are stored in private Azure DNS Zones.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Azure Private DNS Zones for DNS Challenge
steps.description:
Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/issues/5904#issuecomment-1695338997):

>Rotten issues close after 30d of inactivity.
>Reopen the issue with `/reopen`.
>Mark the issue as fresh with `/remove-lifecycle rotten`.
>Send feedback to [jetstack](https://github.com/jetstack).
>/close

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Azure Private DNS Zones for DNS Challenge" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/issues/5904#issuecomment-1695338997):

>Rotten issues close after 30d of inactivity.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
